organizer.name: Community Organizer

climate_energy_planner.name: Climate & Energy Planner

MutualAidHub.name: Mutual Aid Hub

IndependentMediaNetwork.name: Independent Media Network
IndependentMediaNetwork.description: Converts pressure into truth windows and removes disinfo.

LegalClinic.name: Legal Clinic

CommunityMicrogrid.name: Community Microgrid

rest_and_care.name: Rest & Care

cultural_resonance.name: Cultural Resonance

mutual_aid_network.name: Mutual Aid Network

safe_passage.name: Safe Passage

mass_mobilization.name: Mass Mobilization

field_investigation.name: Field Investigation

counter_disinfo.name: Counter Disinfo

truth_window.name: Truth Window

global_expose.name: Global Expose
global_expose.description: Turn evidence into a transregional truth surge.

emergency_injunction.name: Emergency Injunction

clear_aid_corridor.name: Clear Aid Corridor
clear_aid_corridor.description: Reopen humanitarian access and reduce immediate harm.

remedy_dossier.name: Remedy Dossier
remedy_dossier.description: Build the legal case for enforceable remedy.

international_tribunal.name: International Tribunal
international_tribunal.description: Convert legal pressure into a major rights breakthrough.

build_microgrid.name: Build Microgrid
build_microgrid.description: Build local energy resilience in a vulnerable region.

disaster_preparedness.name: Disaster Preparedness
disaster_preparedness.description: Prepare communities before climate shocks land.

resilience_retrofit.name: Resilience Retrofit
resilience_retrofit.description: Strengthen local systems against compounding breakdowns.

rapid_decarbonization.name: Rapid Decarbonization
rapid_decarbonization.description: Force a major shift toward climate protection.

protected_humanitarian_corridors.title: Protected Humanitarian Corridors
protected_humanitarian_corridors.description: Civilian access routes stay open despite escalation.

right_to_information.title: Right To Information
right_to_information.description: Independent reporting and public truth claims are protected.

right_to_remedy.title: Right To Remedy
right_to_remedy.description: Access to remedy becomes enforceable across targeted regions.

climate_reparations_compact.title: Climate Reparations Compact
climate_reparations_compact.description: Resources move toward those living inside the crisis first.

energy_commons.title: Energy Commons
energy_commons.description: Local resilience infrastructure is treated as shared rights work.

cultural_survival_covenant.title: Cultural Survival Covenant
cultural_survival_covenant.description: Culture is defended as a material right, not a luxury.

witness_dignity.name: Witness & Dignity
witness_dignity.description: West Asia & North Africa is under stacked war and rights pressure while climate accelerates the broader crisis.
witness_dignity.introduction: A coalition of organizers, journalists, lawyers, and planners works across a narrowing civic landscape to protect civilians, keep truth moving, and build institutions that can outlast the Capture Engine.
witness_dignity.story: Witness & Dignity keeps Palestine as the moral center of the scenario framing without reducing the region to spectacle. The coalition acts under severe pressure, trying to hold open corridors of care, remedy, and testimony while climate stress compounds every front.
witness_dignity.dramatization: The table should feel like an emergency newsroom, a legal triage room, and a mutual-aid corridor all at once. Every gain is fragile. Every delay has a civilian cost. The scenario is written to keep witness, care, and political clarity in the foreground rather than spectacle.
witness_dignity.gameplay: Witness & Dignity opens under immediate pressure. War, rights, and poverty all threaten to outrun the coalition before long-term institutions can stabilize the map. The strongest line of play is coordinated pacing: protect West Asia & North Africa from spiraling displacement while building enough evidence and solidarity to unlock charter progress.
witness_dignity.mechanics: The scenario rewards public testimony and careful timing. Witness Window cancels the first disinformation placement in a round when the coalition keeps evidence high, while Aid Corridor punishes unchecked war pressure by locking access in West Asia & North Africa. Expect a tighter, more tactical game with fewer safe turns and heavier emphasis on media, legal, and relief sequencing.
witness_dignity.moralCenter: Palestine is framed as the moral center: rights-focused, unsensational, and grounded in civilian protection.

witness_window.label: Witness Window
witness_window.description: If evidence is at least 3, the first disinfo placement each round is cancelled.

aid_corridor.label: Aid Corridor
aid_corridor.description: When WAR pressure reaches 8, West Asia & North Africa gains an AidAccess lock until Organizer or Lawyer clears it.

green_resistance.name: Green Resistance
green_resistance.description: Forest communities face a coordinated extraction surge where climate, energy, and rights pressure reinforce each other.
green_resistance.introduction: The coalition shifts toward land defenders, river communities, and movement journalists trying to hold the line across Abya Yala / Latin America, Africa South of the Sahara, and the wider climate frontier.
green_resistance.story: Green Resistance frames frontline environmental defense as a struggle over memory, sovereignty, and survival. Concessions, private security, and disinformation campaigns clear the way for extraction while local communities fight to keep forests, rivers, and energy futures from becoming sacrifice zones.
green_resistance.dramatization: This scenario should feel slower, wetter, and more territorial than Witness & Dignity. One turn is a blockade on a logging road, the next a testimony circuit, the next a jury-rigged microgrid keeping a village clinic alive while the smoke closes in.
green_resistance.gameplay: Green Resistance begins with more grassroots capacity than Witness & Dignity, but the coalition is spread across more regions and starts with tighter civic space. The challenge is preventing climate and energy escalation from creating a rolling displacement crisis while still investing in culture, legal protection, and long-term infrastructure.
green_resistance.mechanics: Root Solidarity turns cultural protection into momentum by generating solidarity at the start of a round once Art & Culture is stabilized. Extraction Spiral punishes ignored climate pressure by pushing fresh displacement into Abya Yala / Latin America and Africa South of the Sahara before the world phase resolves. The scenario rewards distributed institution-building and punishes leaving ecological fronts to drift.
green_resistance.moralCenter: Indigenous land defenders and forest communities are treated as protagonists with agency, not background scenery for a climate disaster story.

root_solidarity.label: Root Solidarity
root_solidarity.description: At the start of a round, if Art & Culture protection is 5+, gain 1 solidarity.

extraction_spiral.label: Extraction Spiral
extraction_spiral.description: When Climate pressure reaches 7 or more, Abya Yala / Latin America and Africa South of the Sahara each gain 1 displacement during the world phase.

straplines.NorthAmerica: Media power, rights drift, imperial upstream pressure.
straplines.LatinAmerica: Land defense, memory, and solidarity networks.
straplines.Europe: Diplomatic leverage, border regime, legal spillover.
straplines.MENA: War pressure, aid corridors, testimony under siege.
straplines.SouthAsia: Heat stress, energy transitions, mass exposure.
straplines.SoutheastAsia: Disinfo corridors across dense coastal systems.
straplines.PacificIslands: Oceanic front line and climate alarms.

app.restoringBody: The permalink is being resolved into a live session.

status.compromiseVoteLive: Compromise vote is live. Resolve the modal to continue.

home.noScenariosBody: The content registry did not return any playable scenarios.
home.title: Dignity Rising
home.subtitle: A cooperative strategy game about protecting civilians, defending truth, and building institutions under the pressure of a systemic antagonist.
home.savePlaceholder: Paste a serialized save payload here.
home.routesStayStable: Routes stay stable while the selected scenario and active table state change underneath.

overview.title: The Stones Are Crying Out
overview.subtitle: A cooperative board game about solidarity, truth, and resilience in the face of systemic injustice.
overview.whatIsBody1: Dignity Rising is a fully cooperative strategy game where you and your fellow players form a coalition of activists, journalists, lawyers, and climate planners working to protect civilians, defend truth, and build lasting institutions — all while a relentless systemic antagonist (the Capture Engine) pushes the world toward collapse.
overview.whatIsBody2: You win by ratifying clauses in the Global Charter — binding protections for humanitarian corridors, free information, climate reparations, and more. You lose if any of the seven Fronts — War, Climate, Human Rights, Speech & Information, Poverty, Energy, or Art & Culture — collapses beyond recovery.
overview.fantasyBody1: Imagine you're sitting in a dimly lit coordination room. Dispatch reports arrive from the field. Your journalist's last investigation exposed the extraction racket — but now censorship locks are closing fast. The Organizer is preparing a mass mobilization, but the coalition is running dangerously hot on burnout. The Lawyer just filed an emergency injunction at the international level. And the climate clock ticks toward a heatwave that will displace thousands.
overview.fantasyBody2: Every action matters. Every round, the system tightens. Will you accept a compromised deal to buy time, or refuse and absorb the pressure? The stones are crying out. Can you hear them?
overview.pCooperativeBody: You win or lose together. Discussion, coordination, and shared sacrifice define your coalition.
overview.pSystemicBody: No single crisis exists in isolation. War spills into displacement, poverty fuels disinformation, and culture holds it all together.
overview.pNarrativeBody: Every card, every crisis, and every choice is grounded in real-world parallels — from extraction licensing to hospital sieges.
overview.pUrgentBody: The round window is finite. The system escalates automatically. Your coalition must choose where to act and what to sacrifice.

setup.title: Setting Up the Table
setup.subtitle: How to prepare a game of Dignity Rising.
setup.step1Body1: Each scenario frames the crisis differently. Witness & Dignity places intense pressure on the MENA region with war and displacement, while Green Resistance focuses on climate extraction in the Amazon and Sub-Saharan Africa.
setup.step1Body2: Each scenario defines the starting temperature, civic space, resource pool, regional vulnerabilities, and special rules that alter how the game flows.
setup.modeCoreBody: Shorter game with 2 actions per turn. Recommended for first-time players or when you have under 90 minutes.
setup.modeFullBody: Extended game with 3 actions per turn. The full experience with Spotlight cards and deepened escalation.
setup.step3Body1: Each player takes one role. No two players may share the same role. Choose from Community Organizer, Investigative Journalist, Human Rights Lawyer, or Climate & Energy Planner.
setup.step3Body2: Roles determine your unique actions, passive abilities, and the type of institutions you can build. The coalition should discuss which roles are most needed for the chosen scenario.
setup.step4Body1: Before you begin, review the scenario dashboard:
setup.startingTempBody: The global heat level. Rises from crises and the climate clock.
setup.startingCivicBody: Ranges from OPEN to CLOSED. Restricts actions as it narrows.
setup.startingResourcesBody: Solidarity, Evidence, Capacity — your shared resource pool.
setup.startingFrontsBody: Each front begins with starting Pressure, Protection, and Impact values.
setup.startingRegionsBody: Check the World Theatre for vulnerability heatmaps and starting tokens.

phases.title: Round Structure
phases.subtitle: Each round consists of four phases. The system and the coalition take turns shaping the world.
phases.p1Body1: The Capture Engine advances its agenda. A card is drawn from the Capture Engine deck that raises pressure, adds locks, and may offer a compromise deal the coalition must vote on.
phases.p1Body2: Then a Crisis card is drawn, bringing climate disasters, displacement, or escalation. Deterioration hooks also fire — active locks continue to inflict damage automatically.
phases.p1Tip: ⚠️ Tip: Pay attention to coupling rules. When War pressure is ≥7, displacement tokens appear and MENA access locks may trigger. When Poverty ≥7, disinformation floods the map.
phases.p2Body1: Each player takes their turn in seat order. On your turn, you have 2 actions (Core) or 3 actions (Full). Spend them to:
phases.p2Action1: Use role-specific actions that target fronts, regions, or the global state
phases.p2Action2: Play shared actions like Rest & Care or Cultural Resonance
phases.p2Action3: Trigger a breakthrough action at the cost of heavy burnout
phases.p2Body2: Actions may consume resources (Solidarity, Evidence, Capacity) and some require targeting a specific region or front.
phases.p3Body1: Coupling rules fire. The temperature may tick up. Front collapse conditions are checked — if any front's Impact reaches 10, or if extreme conditions (Pressure ≥9 and Protection ≤1) are met, the front collapses and the coalition loses immediately.
phases.p3Body2: Passive resource generation occurs, round flags reset, and delayed effects from earlier compromise deals may trigger.
phases.p4Body1: If the coalition has accumulated enough Charter Progress and the prerequisites for a Charter Clause are met, the clause is automatically ratified — permanently locking in protections and benefits.
phases.p4Body2: The round counter advances. If the maximum number of rounds has been reached, the game ends and your final tier is determined by the number of ratified clauses and remaining front health.

roles.title: Roles & Abilities
roles.subtitle: Each role brings a unique perspective and toolkit to the coalition.
roles.organizerDesc: Builds solidarity and keeps people moving when corridors close.
roles.journalistDesc: Turns testimony into evidence and cracks open truth windows.
roles.lawyerDesc: Clears locks, raises remedy pressure, and protects civic remedies.
roles.plannerDesc: Builds resilient infrastructure that slows climate spillover.
roles.sharedBody: All players have access to these regardless of role:
roles.restCareBody: Recover 2 Burnout. Essential for staying in the fight.
roles.culturalResonanceBody: Spend 1 Solidarity. Draw a Culture card in a region and boost Culture protection.
roles.burnoutBody: Every player has a Burnout gauge. Breakthrough actions cost 2 Burnout. When Burnout is high, your character becomes Strained — certain actions may be locked or penalties applied. At maximum Burnout, you risk a complete incapacitation. Use Rest & Care and Culture cards to recover.

fronts.title: The Seven Fronts
fronts.subtitle: Fronts represent the global battlegrounds your coalition must manage. Each front has three tracks.
fronts.pressureBody: How aggressively the system is pushing against this front. High pressure drives impact upward and triggers coupling rules.
fronts.protectionBody: Your coalition's accumulated defenses. Protections buffer against damage and are prerequisites for Charter ratification.
fronts.impactBody: The real-world harm that has occurred. When Impact reaches 10, the front collapses and you lose.
fronts.systemicTip: 💡 Key Insight: Fronts are interconnected. Ignoring one front will cascade into others. War pressure creates displacement, which strains rights. Poverty fuels disinformation, which undermines speech. Culture is both your shield and your last resort for maintaining solidarity. Think systemically.

regions.title: World Map & Regions
regions.subtitle: The world is divided into eight regions, each with distinct vulnerability profiles.
regions.howBody1: Each region has a vulnerability profile that determines how global pressure translates into local harm. When a crisis card or coupling rule fires, vulnerable regions take the brunt of the damage — receiving displacement tokens, locks, or direct pressure increases.
regions.howBody2: Regions can accumulate tokens (displacement, disinformation, extraction), locks (Aid Access, Censorship, Surveillance), and institutions (Mutual Aid Hubs, Legal Clinics, etc.).
regions.displacementBody: Civilians forced to move. Removed by Organizer actions.
regions.disinfoBody: Propaganda undermining public trust. Removed by Journalist actions.
regions.debtBody: The cost of accepting system deals. Triggers delayed effects.
regions.aidBody: Blocks humanitarian action. Cleared by Lawyer or Organizer.
regions.censorshipBody: Restricts journalism and culture actions. Cleared by Truth Windows.
regions.surveillanceBody: Increases rights pressure every round. Cleared by Lawyer.

resources.title: Resources & Economy
resources.subtitle: The coalition shares a pool of three core resources, plus a fourth gained through deals.
resources.solidarityBody: The people's will to act together. Spent by the Organizer to build Mutual Aid Networks and by anyone to trigger Cultural Resonance. Generated through Community Mobilization and Culture cards.
resources.evidenceBody: Documented testimony, investigations, and accountability records. The Journalist generates it; the Lawyer spends it on injunctions and dossiers. Also a prerequisite for several Charter clauses.
resources.capacityBody: Technical expertise and infrastructure. The Planner spends it to build microgrids and resilience retrofits. Generated passively and through system effects.
resources.reliefBody: Emergency humanitarian aid. Gained primarily through compromise deals offered by the Capture Engine. Useful in the short term, but often comes with hidden costs (Compromise Debt).
resources.scarcityTip: ⚠️ Resource Scarcity: Resources are shared across the entire coalition. In a 4-player game, spending 2 Evidence on an injunction means the Journalist can't counter disinfo this round. Coordinate carefully.

cards.title: Card Decks
cards.subtitle: Three decks drive the game's narrative and mechanical pressure.
cards.captureBody: The systemic antagonist. Each round, a Capture Engine card is drawn that raises pressure, deploys locks, and spreads disinformation. Cards are organized by four pillars:
cards.extraPillars: Some Capture cards offer Compromise Deals — immediate relief at the cost of future debt. The coalition must vote whether to accept or reject.
cards.crisisBody: Natural disasters, escalations, and emergencies that deal direct Impact damage and generate displacement. Crisis cards are drawn each round during the World Phase.
cards.cultureBody: The coalition's creative resistance. Culture cards are drawn through the Cultural Resonance action and provide resources, burnout recovery, truth windows, and front protection.

charter.title: The Global Charter
charter.subtitle: Your coalition's ultimate goal: ratify binding clauses that permanently protect people and institutions.
charter.howBody1: Each Charter clause has prerequisites — specific thresholds your coalition must reach (e.g., "Rights Protection ≥6" or "Evidence ≥5"). When you achieve enough Charter Progress (through the Lawyer's Remedy Dossier or International Tribunal actions), the first available clause that meets its prerequisites is automatically ratified.
charter.howBody2: Ratified clauses provide permanent benefits — locking in protections, resources, and pressure reductions that persist for the rest of the game.

corridors.title: Protected Humanitarian Corridors
corridors.effect: Remove Aid Access locks in MENA, +1 Rights Protection

info.title: Right To Information
info.effect: Remove Censorship locks globally, −1 Speech Pressure

winning.title: Winning & Losing
winning.subtitle: The coalition's fate is determined by their Charter progress and the state of the world.
winning.victoryBody: The game ends after the final round. Your ending tier is determined by the number of ratified Charter clauses and the overall health of the seven Fronts.

gold.label: GOLD
gold.desc: All 6 clauses ratified. The world is transformed.

silver.label: SILVER
silver.desc: 4–5 clauses ratified. Major progress amid pain.

bronze.label: BRONZE
bronze.desc: 2–3 clauses ratified. A fragile peace holds.

winning.defeatBody: The coalition loses immediately if any of these occur:
winning.defeat1: Any Front's Impact reaches 10 — catastrophic, irreversible harm.
winning.defeat2: Any Front's Pressure reaches 9+ AND Protection reaches 0–1 — undefended collapse.
winning.defeat3: The Temperature reaches critical levels with no remaining climate buffer.
winning.defeat4: Civic Space reaches CLOSED — all organized resistance is crushed.
winning.strategy: 💡 Strategy: Watch the coupling rules. A front that looks "fine" can cascade into collapse through interconnected pressure. Prioritize the most vulnerable front while maintaining minimum protection on all others.

glossary.title: Glossary
glossary.subtitle: Quick reference for key game terms and mechanics.

burnout.def: A per-player gauge that increases when using breakthrough actions. High burnout strains the player, potentially locking actions.

capture.def: The systemic antagonist. Automatically escalates pressure each round through extraction, militarization, control, and manufactured consent.

charter.def: The Global Charter — a collection of clauses that the coalition can ratify for permanent protections.

charterProgress.def: A cumulative counter advanced by Remedy Dossier and similar actions. Used to trigger automatic ratification.

civicSpace.def: A global state ranging from OPEN to CLOSED. As it narrows, player actions become more restricted.

compromise.def: An offer from the Capture Engine that provides immediate benefit at the cost of future harm (Compromise Debt).

coupling.def: A systemic link between fronts. When one front reaches a threshold, it triggers effects on other fronts or regions.

deterioration.def: Automatic damage that fires each round when certain locks are active (e.g., Aid Access locks cause Rights Impact).

displacement.def: A token representing civilians forced to move. Accumulates in regions and can be removed by Organizer actions.

disinfo.def: A token representing propaganda. Accumulates in regions and is removed by Journalist counter-disinfo actions.

front.def: One of seven global battlegrounds (War, Climate, Rights, Speech, Poverty, Energy, Culture) tracked by Pressure, Protection, and Impact.

impact.def: The accumulated real-world harm on a front. Reaching 10 causes collapse and immediate defeat.

institution.def: A persistent structure built in a region (e.g., Mutual Aid Hub, Legal Clinic). Provides ongoing benefits but can be damaged.

lock.def: A binary restriction on a region (Aid Access, Censorship, Surveillance) that causes ongoing harm until removed.

pressure.def: How aggressively the system pushes against a front. High pressure triggers coupling rules and drives impact.

protection.def: The coalition's accumulated defenses on a front. Buffers against damage and enables Charter ratification.

temperature.def: The global climate clock. Rises from crises and captures. Used in climate-related checks.

truth.def: A round-scoped flag on a region that allows journalism and culture actions to bypass censorship and gain bonuses.

scenarioBooklet.title: Stories, rules, and table texture
scenarioBooklet.subtitle: Read each dossier like a spread from the campaign guide, then carry the selected chapter straight into setup.

game.temperatureDetail: Band {{band}}. {{count}} crisis card{{plural}} on the next world resolution.
game.civicSpaceDetail: Tracks how much organizing room remains for the coalition.
game.roundControlBody: Advance only when the table is ready. The current session is always linkable in-place.
game.adoptResolutionLead: Cards are on the table. Review the crisis slot, then adopt the resolution.
game.drawResolutionLead: Open the crisis deck, reveal the current draw, and place the active card on the board.
game.pendingDrawBody: Draw the world cards to place the current capture docket on the board.

debug.title: Debug
debug.autoplayIdle: Ready to run scripted rounds through the normal game flow.
debug.autoplayProgress: Executing {{phase}}. {{count}} round{{plural}} left in the queue.
debug.autoplayStalled: Autoplay stopped because no legal next command was available.
debug.autoplayFailed: Autoplay stopped because the next command failed.
debug.autoplayWon: Autoplay stopped because the coalition reached a win state.
debug.autoplayLost: Autoplay stopped because the coalition collapsed.
